response.justification: Szabo is an exceptional engineer with 30+ years of experience and a proven track record building complex systems at scale, far exceeding the 15-year requirement. However, the role explicitly requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI,' and the resume shows no meaningful AI/ML product development history. The recent mention of MCP and AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq is a positive signal but appears superficially covered. His leadership skills and technical breadth are strong, but the gap in demonstrated AI expertise is substantial for a director-level AI strategy role.
strengths: Vast technical foundation: 30+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure with expertise in multiple languages and paradigms
Proven architectural skills: Built scalable micro-services handling millions of requests/second and platforms adopted by real organizations (Green Map, GISCollective)
Strong technical leadership: Staff-level engineer, organized recurring Refactoring Dojos, created multiple public workshops, speaks at major conferences (FOSS4G)
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML product expertise: Resume shows no history of building AI products, implementing ML models, or AI-focused engineering work before the recent MCP mention
Limited cloud platform strategy background: Career focused on full-stack development rather than cloud architecture, platform strategy, or infrastructure design decisions
No evidence of advising on AI/cloud adoption: Role requires advising strategic customers on cutting-edge AI/cloud technologies; resume shows no such customer-facing advisory experience

key_factors.factor: Technical foundation and years of experience
